items.title: Another week, more progress for Colts’ Daniel Jones
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Daniel Jones’ rehab continued to progress without a hiccup. The Indianapolis Colts’ starting quarterback saw his first action in 7-on-7 work during Monday’s organized team activities (OTAs) practice. That was the next anticipated step as Jones recovers from Dec. 9 surgery to repair a torn right Achilles tendon. Prior to Monday, Jones’ participation [...]

items.summary: Jones is nearing the six-month mark from sustaining a torn Achilles against the Jacksonville Jaguars in Week 14 of the 2025 season.

items.title: Colts Hall of Famer Raymond Berry dies
items.summary: Berry spent all 13 years of his NFL career with the Baltimore Colts and was inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame in 1973.

items.title: Colts legend, Hall of Famer Raymond Berry dies at 93
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Colts and NFL have lost a legend and a gentleman. Raymond Berry, a trail-blazing receiver with the Baltimore Colts, has died. He was 93. Berry’s family made the announcement Monday in a message to the Pro Football Hall of Fame. Berry was a two-time world champion with the Colts and teamed with [...]

items.title: Colts' 'Kicking The Stigma' marks 1 year since Jim Irsay's death
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS -- It was on May 21, 2025, that Indianapolis Colts Owner Jim Irsay died in Southern California. Irsay had always been public about his struggle to stay clean and had donated $32 million since 2020 to “Kicking the Stigma," a campaign that acknowledges the challenges of mental health wellness and funds treatment and research. items.title: Colts headed to London in NFL's international slate
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts are headed overseas. Again. The NFL announced Wednesday morning the Colts will meet the Washington Commanders Oct. 4 in London’s 62,850-seat Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Week 4 matchup will be one of their nine away games. The team confirmed the news on social media. Kickoff is set for 9:30 a.m. [...]
